MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed. The court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Proceedings not reported. (212)

RONALD LEW, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS CALLED TWICE. THE COURT ASKED THE PARTIES TO GO OUTSIDE THE COURTROOM AND SEE IF THEY COULD REACH A SETTLEMENT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT RONALD LEW'S Motion For Reconsideration Of, Or Relief From Order On Cross-Defendant's Special Motion To Strike Cross-Complainant'S Cross-Complaint Pursuant To CCP 425.16. MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILS TO SUBMIT EVIDENCE OF NEW FACTS OR LAW NOT AVAILABLE AT EARLIER HEARING. MOTION FOR RELIEF UNDER CCP 473 (B) IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH REQUIREMENTS OF THE STATUTE. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- RONALD LEW, TEL. NO. (650) 949-3888, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - NOTICE WAS NOT GIVEN TO THE OTHER PARTY AFTER THE TENTATIVE RULING CAME OUT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS-DEFENDANT BERNARD LEW'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE THE CROSS COMPLAINT PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 42516 IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED.
